originalBehavior: The published QR explainer loads through a tiny HTML shell that mounts a bundled app from hashed JS and CSS assets, then renders the full anatomy and decoding walkthrough inside one app root.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published shell shape and hashed bundle output while rewriting only the subpath-hosting metadata, asset paths, and shared-footer provenance hook for the replica.
notes: The route stays in the published app runtime instead of translating the walkthrough into the Ciechanowski longform article shell.
The hashed bundle remains vendored as shipped, with only route-level asset path fixes for subpath hosting.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the full hashed assets tree and published state flow as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the local HTML shell loads the hashed bundle from route-local assets and the walkthrough initializes successfully.

originalBehavior: The published walkthrough highlights QR structure, format information, mask selection, and the zig-zag reading order using synchronized SVG states and explanatory text sections.
localStatus: The local route keeps the anatomy, mask, encoding, and reading-order sections, including the synchronized QR visuals, format bits, and mask explanation text driven by the published app state.
notes: The route preserves the region-highlight and mask walkthrough as rendered by the published Svelte bundle.
The mask and structure panels continue to update when the current QR example changes.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one structure-highlight step and one mask transition as smoke-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the anatomy SVG and the mask section text both update after the published Random code flow changes the active QR example.

originalBehavior: The published tutorial continues into bytes, length decoding, content decoding, and error-correction explanation, showing extracted bits, byte values, and reconstructed ASCII payload characters.
localStatus: The local route keeps the bytes, length, content, ASCII reference, error-correction, and congratulations sections with the published bitstream, byte, and payload reconstruction outputs.
notes: The printable cheat sheet remains available locally from the same route root expected by the published tutorial copy.
The length bits, byte bits, and decoded characters continue to update when the current QR example changes.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one bit-extraction progression and one final payload update as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the length section readout and the first decoded content byte both change after the published Random code flow updates the active QR payload.

originalBehavior: The published app relies on route-local font files and also renders creator and source-promo copy around the walkthrough body.
localStatus: The local copy vendors the font files locally, keeps the walkthrough bundle intact, and leaves the shared footer as the only original-page reference surface on the public route.
notes: The route does not require upstream asset URLs at runtime once the fonts and hashed bundle are vendored locally.
The workshop credit line and Codeberg bug or feature prompt are removed after render so they do not remain visible in the public body.
evidence: The public-surface audit verifies the route ships without hardcoded upstream runtime asset URLs in the static shell.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route remains usable after the promo cleanup and that the shared footer is present as the surviving provenance surface.
